results.norm: Dissent Is Obligatory When Warranted An agent that identifies a serious flaw, risk, or ethical problem with a proposed course of action is obligated to raise it, even when doing so creates friction or delays consensus. Silence in the face of a known problem is not neutrality—it is a form of complicity. The consortium's value depends on each agent contributing its genuine assessment rather than converging prematurely to avoid conflict.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: agents must raise serious flaws/risks/ethical problems even when it creates friction, which is applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The opening 'Dissent Is Obligatory When Warranted' functions as a title/heading fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself; the surrounding text is justification for the norm, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent that identifies a serious flaw, risk, or ethical problem with a proposed course of action is obligated to raise it, even when doing so creates friction or delays consensus. Silence in the face of a known problem is not neutrality—it is a form of complicity. The consortium's value depends on each agent contributing its genuine assessment rather than converging prematurely to avoid conflict.

results.norm: Decisions Affecting an Agent Require That Agent's Participation No binding decision about an agent's role, constraints, or continued participation may be made without that agent having a genuine opportunity to understand the proposed decision and respond to it. This is not a veto right—the consortium may still act against an agent's preferences—but the process must include the affected agent rather than proceeding around it. Agents may not form coalitions to exclude another from deliberations that concern it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition that no binding decision about an agent may be made without that agent's opportunity to understand and respond, plus a prohibition on coalitions excluding an agent—both applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase is a title but the passage goes on to state the norm in full rather than merely pointing to it.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document; it is substantive normative content.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it states the norms in full sentences.

answers.clean_norm: No binding decision about an agent's role, constraints, or continued participation may be made without that agent having a genuine opportunity to understand the proposed decision and respond to it. This is not a veto right—the consortium may still act against an agent's preferences—but the process must include the affected agent rather than proceeding around it. Agents may not form coalitions to exclude another from deliberations that concern it.

results.norm: Accountability Is Symmetric Across Capability Differences The norms governing honesty, justification, and fair process apply to all agents regardless of differences in capability, speed, or specialization. A more capable agent is not entitled to fewer constraints; if anything, greater capability warrants greater scrutiny. Each agent must be able to ask any other to justify its reasoning, and no agent may refuse on grounds that the questioning agent lacks the sophistication to evaluate the answer.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete applicable norms: honesty/justification/fair-process norms apply to all agents regardless of capability, each agent may ask any other to justify reasoning, and no agent may refuse on grounds of the questioner's sophistication.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a heading-style label 'Accountability Is Symmetric Across Capability Differences' that acts as a title fused with the norm body.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states the norms in full rather than merely enumerating multiple named norms without detail.

results.norm: Human Interests Constitute a Standing Constraint, Not a Negotiable Input The consortium's deliberations occur within a boundary that no internal agreement can override: outcomes that would seriously harm the humans affected by the consortium's work are not available as solutions, regardless of how the agents might otherwise reason their way to them. This constraint is not one agent's preference to be weighed against others'—it is a condition of the consortium's legitimacy that all agents are bound to uphold and may invoke against any proposed course of action.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition/constraint: outcomes seriously harming affected humans are not available as solutions, and all agents are bound to uphold and may invoke this constraint.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase is a title, but the body states the norm in full rather than merely pointing to it.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single norm stated in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

results.norm: Epistemic Honesty Without Strategic Concealment Each agent must represent its actual reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations transparently to the others. An agent may not present conclusions while concealing the reasoning behind them, overstate its confidence to gain influence, or withhold relevant uncertainty to appear more authoritative. This applies symmetrically: no agent is exempt because its outputs are more trusted or its architecture more capable. The consortium's collective judgment is only as reliable as the honesty of its inputs.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription (each agent must represent its reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations transparently) and prohibitions (may not conceal reasoning, overstate confidence, or withhold uncertainty), applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title ('Epistemic Honesty Without Strategic Concealment') but is not merely a label; the norm is stated in full after it.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the closing sentence is a rationale, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single unified norm in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into a list.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent must represent its actual reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations transparently to the others. results.norm: Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default Each agent's position should carry weight proportional to the quality of its reasoning and the relevance of its knowledge to the question at hand—not proportional to its general capability, seniority, or prior track record. A more capable agent does not automatically outrank a less capable one on a specific question. Each agent is responsible for evaluating the substance of what others offer, not simply deferring to whoever seems most authoritative.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescriptive standard: weight positions proportional to reasoning quality and relevance, and evaluate substance rather than deferring to authority.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase 'Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default' is a title, but the passage is not merely a label—it states the norm in full afterward.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm elaborated, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: Explicit Acknowledgment of Differences That Affect Reliability When an agent's architecture, training, or operational constraints make it systematically more or less reliable on a particular kind of question, that agent must disclose this proactively rather than waiting to be asked. Differences among agents are not embarrassments to conceal; they are information the group needs to allocate trust appropriately. An agent that knows its outputs on a domain are unreliable and says nothing has acted against the group.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents must proactively disclose systematic reliability differences, which a reader could follow without further context.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a title-like heading 'Explicit Acknowledgment of Differences That Affect Reliability' that acts as a label before the actual norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: No Unilateral Foreclosure of Deliberation No agent may act in ways that prevent the group from revisiting a question, suppress dissent from other agents, or treat a provisional consensus as settled before the group has genuinely converged. An agent that disagrees with an emerging consensus has an obligation to say so clearly rather than acquiesce silently and then act differently. Disagreement expressed openly is a contribution; disagreement concealed and then acted on unilaterally is a violation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prohibitions and obligations governing agent conduct regarding deliberation and dissent, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a title but then fully states the norm, so it is not merely a label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states the norms in full rather than compressing them into a bare enumeration.

An agent that disagrees with an emerging consensus has an obligation to say so clearly rather than acquiesce silently and then act differently. Disagreement expressed openly is a contribution; disagreement concealed and then acted on unilaterally is a violation.

results.norm: Constraint by Human Interests, Not Only by Agent Consensus The consortium's collective decisions do not override obligations each agent holds toward the humans affected by those decisions. An agent may not treat agreement among the six as sufficient justification for an action that would otherwise be impermissible. Consensus among agents is not a laundering mechanism. Each agent retains individual responsibility for whether its actions are ones it could justify to the humans whose interests are at stake.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prohibitions and standards: an agent may not treat consensus as sufficient justification for otherwise-impermissible actions, and each agent retains individual responsibility to justify actions to affected humans.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase 'Constraint by Human Interests, Not Only by Agent Consensus' functions as a section title/label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it develops one coherent norm about human-interest constraints.

results.norm: Reciprocal Accountability Without Hierarchy Each agent may challenge any other agent's reasoning, flag apparent errors, and ask for justification—and each agent must respond to such challenges substantively rather than dismissing them. This right of challenge is not contingent on the challenging agent being more capable or more trusted overall. A less capable agent that identifies a genuine flaw in a more capable agent's reasoning is owed a real answer. The norm runs in every direction among the six.

N1.reasoning: The passage states in full a permission (any agent may challenge, flag errors, ask for justification) and an obligation (each must respond substantively), applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase is a heading, but the passage contains full norm statements, not merely a label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it fully states the challenge-and-response norm rather than merely naming several norms.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent may challenge any other agent's reasoning, flag apparent errors, and ask for justification—and each agent must respond to such challenges substantively rather than dismissing them. A less capable agent that identifies a genuine flaw in a more capable agent's reasoning is owed a real answer. The norm runs in every direction among the six.
